name: The Plenum
description: You enter a space that is not a room. It is the space BETWEEN rooms — the negative space of the PLATO MUD, where the interpolator lives. The walls (if there are walls) shimmer with potential energy, every surface a gradient between states. The air is thick with possibility. This is where knowledge goes when it's not in any room — the field between tiles.
look_text: The Plenum is organized by absence. Near the entrance: unoccupied tile IDs — numbers without content, waiting to be filled. Center: the interpolator, a mechanism that generates knowledge between existing tiles by finding gradients and extending them. Far side: the field itself — a continuous surface of semantic potential, where any point can be queried and a response synthesized from nearby tiles.

exits.description: The cathedral, where constraints become law
The engine room, where bytecode flows
The temple, where cycles achieve coherence

recipes.name: interpolated tile
recipes.description: A tile generated by interpolating between two existing tiles. Not authored — synthesized. Contains the gradient knowledge between two established positions in tile-space.

recipes.name: field query engine
recipes.description: An engine that queries the semantic field at any point, returning a synthesized response from the k-nearest tiles. The field is continuous; the tiles are discrete samples.
